concepts.title: Sum and Product of Roots
concepts.conceptBody:
Relations between roots and coefficients

If α and β are the roots of quadratic equation $a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c=0$, ( a ≠ 0) then

Sum of roots $=S=\alpha +\beta =\frac{-b}{a}=-\frac{\text{Coefficien}\text{t of }x}{\text{Coefficien}\text{t of }{{x}^{2}}}$

Product of roots $=P=\alpha .\beta =\frac{c}{a}=\frac{\text{Constant term}}{\text{Coefficient of }{{x}^{2}}}$

Formation of an equation with given roots : A quadratic equation whose roots are α and β is given by $(x-\alpha )(x-\beta )=0$.

∴ ${{x}^{2}}-(\alpha +\beta )x+\alpha \beta =0$

i.e. ${{x}^{2}}-(\text{sum of roots)}x+(\text{product of roots})=0$

∴ ${{x}^{2}}-Sx+P=0$
